title: hernandia ovigera subsp. stokesii
intro: Hernandia ovigera subsp. stokesii, also known as the yellow water wort, is a tropical plant species that belongs to the Hernandiaceae family. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, such as treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as acting as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of Hernandia ovigera subsp. stokesii include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
